progress.scanning: Scanning devices from network, this may take 5 to 15 seconds

retry.title: No DEFA Balancer found
retry.description:
No DEFA Balancer found on the network.

Verify:
- Device is powered on
- Device is connected to the network
- Device is on the same subnet/VLAN as Home Assistant
- Multicast UDP traffic is not blocked (check your router configuration)
- Restart the device by turning its power off and on

already_configured.title: No new DEFA Balancer found
already_configured.description: Discovered DEFA Balancer devices are already configured in Home Assistant.

select.title: DEFA Balancer found
select.description: Found {count} DEFA Balancer(s) on the network. Select the device to set up.

connection_error.title: Connection error
connection_error.description:
Home Assistant could not start multicast listening.

Check network and host settings, then try again.

reconfigure.title: DEFA Balancer {serial}
reconfigure.description: DEFA Balancer {serial} uses fixed multicast address and requires no configuration.

error.cannot_connect: Unable to open the multicast socket. Check that multicast UDP traffic is allowed on the network.

data_description.phase_voltage: Voltage per phase in volts (V), constrained to 100–250. Default is 230V. This is used to calculate power from current readings. Note, that actual real-world voltage may vary depending on time, location and condition of the electrical grid, so power calculation are only approximate.

device_unreachable.message: No data received from DEFA Balancer – check device is powered on and reachable.

device_unreachable.title: Device unreachable
device_unreachable.description:
The DEFA Balancer has stopped sending data to Home Assistant. Check the following:
- Device is powered on
- Device is connected to the network
- Device is on the same subnet/VLAN as Home Assistant
- Multicast UDP traffic is not blocked
- Restart the device by turning its power off and on

The issue will clear automatically after data starts arriving again.
